title: Fix: Safe grave number change, consistent alley sorting, prevent data loss

requirements.summary: Apply consistent alley sorting on the frontend in all components that render alleys so the UI always shows a predictable alphabetical/numerical order
acceptanceCriteria: AdminGraveTileMap renders alleys in alphabetical/numerical order
GraveTileMap renders alleys in alphabetical/numerical order
CemeteryLayoutManager displays alleys in alphabetical/numerical order
Restoring or adding an alley immediately places it in the correct sorted position in all views without requiring a page reload

file_operations.description: Add client-side alley sorting before rendering. Sort the cemetery.alleys array alphabetically by name (with numeric fallback) immediately after receiving data from useGetCemeteryState. Apply a stable sort function that handles both alphabetic and numeric alley names consistently.

Add client-side alley sorting before rendering. Sort the cemetery.alleys array alphabetically by name (with numeric fallback) immediately after receiving data from useGetPublicTiles. Use the same sorting logic as AdminGraveTileMap to ensure consistency across public and admin views.

Add client-side alley sorting before rendering. Sort the cemetery.alleys array alphabetically by name (with numeric fallback) immediately after receiving data from useGetCemeteryState. Ensure the sorted order is maintained after add/remove operations by re-sorting on each render.

Add client-side alley sorting for any alley grouping or display logic. If the component renders alley names or groups results by alley, apply the same alphabetical/numerical sort as other components to maintain consistency.

Create a utility module exporting a sortAlleys function that takes an array of AlleyView objects and returns a sorted copy. Implement logic that first attempts numeric comparison (if alley names parse as numbers) and falls back to locale-aware alphabetical comparison. Include a naturalSort helper for mixed alphanumeric alley names (e.g., 'Alley 2' before 'Alley 10'). Export the function for reuse across all alley-rendering components.

requirements.summary: Add a safeguard in GraveEditDialog that warns the admin before saving a plot number change, showing a confirmation step specifically for plot number changes to prevent accidental data-loss operations
acceptanceCriteria: When the admin changes the plot number field and clicks Save, a confirmation dialog appears before the save is executed
The confirmation message explicitly mentions that the plot/grave number is being changed
Cancelling the confirmation leaves all data unchanged
Confirming proceeds with the safe atomic update from REQ-1

file_operations.description: Add plotNumber change detection by tracking the original plotNumber when the dialog opens (for edit mode). Before calling the save mutation, compare the current plotNumber field value with the original. If they differ, show a confirmation AlertDialog that explicitly warns the admin that changing the grave number will update the grave identifier. The confirmation message should state: 'You are about to change the plot number from [old] to [new]. This will update the grave identifier. Do you want to continue?' Include Cancel and Confirm buttons. Only proceed with the save mutation if the user confirms. If plotNumber has not changed, skip the confirmation and save immediately as before.
